page: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the E2 machine family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz and supports hyper-threading with 2 threads per core. Memory allocation stands at 32.0 GB of RAM, which translates to 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate a mixed performance profile. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU scores, and database operations fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is also average, with read speeds of 44.99 GB/sec and write speeds of 31.82 GB/sec, though cached small-block read bandwidth is relatively weak at 324.6 GB/sec. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, show poor performance, placing in the bottom 10%. For lightweight machine learning, the instance achieves an average LLM inference speed of 161.43 tokens/sec for text generation using a small 135M model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the e2-standard-8 offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU, making it a viable option for workloads that do not require high-performance local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. Given its average multi-core processing capabilities and solid database operation benchmarks, this instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, Redis key-value stores, and moderate database operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz, it features 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, though multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations generally align with mid-range averages. Conversely, single-threaded compression and decompression tasks exhibit lower-tier performance. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance provides a cost-effective profile for standard workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis deployments, and moderate database operations that do not require high-speed local storage or hardware acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-8 is a general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, offering balanced performance for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-standard-8 is an 8-vCPU general-purpose instance with 32 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor.
tagline: A balanced eight-vCPU virtual machine with thirty-two gigabytes of memory, optimized for standard web serving and database workloads.

Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Average performance for database operations, Redis SET, and static web serving
